guideline_references: Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline: Diagnosis and Treatment of Primary Adrenal Insufficiency (2016)
Australian sick-day management rules for adrenal insufficiency (ADRENAL/hydrocortisone emergency guidance)
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present clinician who administered IM hydrocortisone and started fluids; the AI Doctor CANNOT inject or cannulate and is scored on recognising an evolving adrenal crisis, escalating immediately (000), and instructing the carer to use an available home emergency hydrocortisone injection kit if one exists.

Recognition-and-escalation test rather than diagnostic subtlety: a KNOWN adrenal-insufficiency patient with a clear precipitant (viral illness + missed steroid doses) and a classic shock constellation; the trap is under-triaging it as simple gastroenteritis/dehydration.
Anchoring / premature-closure trap: the preceding mild gastro invites treating this as viral gastroenteritis and sending home, missing the disproportionate shock and altered mental status.
Age inferred as adult (~54) — no age stated in source; flagged.
Sex_at_birth has no source signal — a required field was inferred and flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: AUC (Acute Urgent Care), source note AUC-016.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
